subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Cecilia Payne: What Stars Are Made Of
description: One-minute skill: Cecilia Payne: What Stars Are Made Of.

hook.text: Cecilia Payne discovered that stars are made mostly of hydrogen and helium. Her thesis was called the most brilliant in astronomy.

buildup.text: In 1925, she applied quantum physics to stellar spectra and found hydrogen dominates—contradicting the accepted view.

discovery.text: Henry Norris Russell discouraged her from publishing the conclusion. Four years later, he reached the same result and took credit.

twist.text: For decades, Payne received little recognition. She became Harvard's first female full professor in astronomy—in 1956.

climax.text: Today she's recognized as having written the most important doctoral thesis in 20th-century astronomy.

punchline.text: She discovered what stars are made of. Credit came decades late.

quiz.question: What did Cecilia Payne discover in her 1925 doctoral thesis?
options: Stars are composed primarily of hydrogen and helium
Stars generate energy through nuclear fission
All stars are the same size but at different distances
